title: Draco glomerata
taxonomy: Draco glomerata is classified within the family of flowering plants, specifically in the genus Draco and the order Asparagales. It is a species of flowering plant, native to the Himalayan region and parts of China. The classification of Draco glomerata is further detailed with the family name Asparagaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the asparagus family. The genus name Draco is derived from the Greek word for 'dragon', likely due to the shape of its leaves. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Draco glomerata, also known as the St. John's wort, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and flowers contain bioactive compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects.
